perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Association will use their best efforts to cause any post-effective amendment to the Registration Statement to be declared effective by the Commission and any post-effective amendment to the Conversion Application to be approved by the OTS and will immediately upon receipt of any information concerning the events listed below notify Capital Resources and promptly confirm the notice in writing:
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Association will endeavor to cause any post-effective amendment to the Registration Statement to be considered by the Commission and any post-effective amendment to the Conversion Application to be reviewed by the OTS and will attempt to notify Capital Resources upon awareness of information concerning the events listed below; notification details will follow when possible:
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'best efforts' with 'endeavor' and 'attempt' introduces ambiguity regarding the level of commitment required. Changing 'declared effective' to 'considered' and 'approved' to 'reviewed' weakens the obligation to achieve specific regulatory outcomes. Adding 'notification details will follow when possible' further reduces the certainty and immediacy of required notifications.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts 17 CFR § 230.473, which implicitly requires diligence in filing amendments to registration statements. The SEC requires prompt and accurate filings, and the ambiguous language weakens the commitment to ensure timely and effective amendments, thus potentially delaying important information to investors.
perturbation.location: SECTION 6. Covenants of the Company and Association.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 230.473 - Delaying amendments. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.473 - Delaying amendments. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 230.473 Delaying amendments. (a) An amendment in the following form filed with a registration statement, or as an amendment to a registration statement which has not become effective, shall be deemed, for the purpose of section 8(a) of the Act , to be filed on such date or dates as may be necessary to delay the effective date of such registration statement (1) until the registrant shall file a further amendment which specifically states as provided in paragraph (b) of this section that such registration statement shall thereafter become effective in accordance with section 8(a) of the Act , or (2) until the registration statement shall become effective on such date as the Commission , acting pursuant to section 8(a), may determine: The registrant hereby amends this registration statement on such date or dates as may be necessary to delay its effective date until the registrant shall file a further amendment which specifically states that this registration statement shall thereafter become effective in accordance with section 8(a) of the Securities Act of 1933 or until the registration statement shall become effective on such date as the Commission acting pursuant to said section 8(a), may determine. (b) An amendment which for the purpose of paragraph (a)(1) of this section specifically states that a registration statement shall thereafter become effective in accordance with section 8(a) of the Act , shall be in the following form: This registration statement shall hereafter become effective in accordance with the provisions of section 8(a) of the Securities Act of 1933 . (c) An amendment pursuant to paragraph (a) of this section which is filed with a registration statement shall be set forth on the facing page thereof. Any
perturbation.justification: The change from "best efforts" to "endeavor" and "attempt," along with weakening the language around regulatory outcomes and notifications, introduces ambiguity. According to the prompt definition, ambiguities in legal statements can lead to legal contradictions, especially when they make it difficult to enforce obligations. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that the ambiguous language weakens the commitment to timely and effective amendments, which implicitly contradicts the need for diligence in filing amendments. While the provided scraped snippets don't directly address the 'best efforts' clause, they do reference 17 CFR § 230.473, related to delaying amendments. The perturbation explanation states that these specific modifications weaken the obligations, which contradicts the implicit requirements of 17 CFR § 230.473.

perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Association will comply in all material respects with any and all terms, conditions, requirements and provisions with respect to the Conversion and the transactions contemplated thereby imposed by the Commission, by applicable state law and regulations, and by the 1933 Act, the 1934 Act and the rules and regulations of the Commission promulgated under such statutes, to be complied with prior to or subsequent to the Closing Date and when the Offering Prospectus is required to be delivered, the Company and the Association will comply in all material respects, at their own expense, with all requirements imposed upon them by the OTS, the Conversion Regulations, the FDIC, the Commission, by applicable state law and regulations and by the 1933 Act, the 1934 Act and the rules and regulations of the Commission promulgated under such statutes, including, without limitation, Rule 10b-6 under the 1934 Act, in each case as from time to time in force, so far as necessary to permit the continuance of sales or dealing in shares of Common Stock during such period in accordance with the provisions hereof and the Offering Prospectus.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Association intend to comply with the applicable terms, conditions, requirements and provisions with respect to the Conversion, as understood and interpreted by their legal counsel, and the transactions contemplated thereby imposed by the Commission, by applicable state law and regulations, and by the 1933 Act, the 1934 Act and the rules and regulations of the Commission promulgated under such statutes, to be complied with prior to or subsequent to the Closing Date and when the Offering Prospectus is required to be delivered, the Company and the Association will try to comply, at their own expense, with the major requirements imposed upon them by the OTS, the Conversion Regulations, the FDIC, the Commission, by applicable state law and regulations and by the 1933 Act, the 1934 Act and the rules and regulations of the Commission promulgated under such statutes, including, without limitation, Rule 10b-6 under the 1934 Act, in each case as from time to time in force, as deemed appropriate to permit the continuance of sales or dealing in shares of Common Stock during such period in accordance with the provisions hereof and the Offering Prospectus.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will comply in all material respects' to 'intend to comply with the applicable terms, conditions, requirements and provisions with respect to the Conversion, as understood and interpreted by their legal counsel' introduces subjectivity and allows for selective compliance based on legal counsel's interpretation. Replacing 'will comply in all material respects' with 'will try to comply, at their own expense, with the major requirements' and adding 'as deemed appropriate' creates ambiguity around which requirements will be followed and who makes that determination.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text weakens the obligation to fully comply with securities laws, specifically 15 U.S. Code § 77e (Section 5 of the Securities Act of 1933), which requires registration of securities offerings. By introducing ambiguity and subjectivity, the change contradicts the law's intent to ensure full compliance with registration requirements for investor protection.
perturbation.location: SECTION 6. Covenants of the Company and Association.

Prohibitions relating to interstate commerce and the mails (a) Sale or delivery after sale of unregistered securities Unless a registration statement is in effect as to a security, it shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly- (1) to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce or
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'compliance in all material respects,' which is a strong obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating the parties 'intend to comply...as understood and interpreted by their legal counsel' and 'will try to comply...with the major requirements...as deemed appropriate.' This introduces subjectivity and discretion, creating ambiguity regarding which requirements will be followed and by whom. This contradicts the intent of securities laws like 15 U.S. Code § 77e (Section 5 of the Securities Act of 1933), as highlighted in the Law Explanation, which requires full compliance with registration requirements to protect investors. While the snippets themselves don't directly quote the 'compliance' requirement, they establish the existence of the Securities Act of 1933 and its registration requirements, which the altered text undermines through ambiguity, satisfying the legal contradiction criteria for 'Ambiguous Legal Obligation.'

perturbation.original_text: The liquidation account for the benefit of account holders with account balances of $50 or more as of the applicable record dates will be duly established and maintained in accordance with the requirements of the OTS, and such Eligible Account Holders and Supplemental Eligible Account Holders who continue to maintain their savings accounts in the Association will have an inchoate interest -12- in their pro rata portion of the liquidation account which shall have a priority superior to that of the holders of shares of Common Stock in the event of a complete liquidation of the Association.
perturbation.changed_text: A liquidation account for the benefit of account holders with account balances of $50 or more as of the applicable record dates may be established and generally maintained in a manner that attempts to align with the suggestions of the OTS, and Eligible Account Holders and Supplemental Eligible Account Holders who continue to maintain their savings accounts in the Association may have a potential interest in their approximate portion of a potential liquidation account which might have a priority that may be considered superior to that of the holders of shares of Common Stock if the Association undergoes a complete liquidation.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be duly established and maintained in accordance with the requirements of the OTS' to 'may be established and generally maintained in a manner that attempts to align with the suggestions of the OTS' weakens the obligation and introduces uncertainty about the account's existence and adherence to regulations. Changing 'will have an inchoate interest' to 'may have a potential interest' further diminishes the certainty of account holder rights. Introducing caveats around the 'portion', 'priority' and 'liquidation' of funds makes all the obligations ambiguous.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text undermines the regulatory requirements outlined in 12 CFR § 563b.40 regarding the establishment and maintenance of a liquidation account for eligible account holders during a conversion from mutual to stock form. The ambiguity about establishing and maintaining the account, along with the uncertainty surrounding the account holders' interest and priority, directly contradicts the clear mandate of the regulation to protect account holders' rights in the event of liquidation.
perturbation.location: SECTION 6. Covenants of the Company and Association.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses definitive language ('will be duly established,' 'will have an inchoate interest'), creating clear obligations. The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens these obligations significantly by using terms like 'may be established,' 'attempts to align,' 'may have a potential interest,' and 'might have a priority.' This directly contradicts the intent of regulations designed to protect account holders during liquidation, by undermining the certainty of the liquidation account's existence, the account holders' interest, and the priority of their claims. While the provided snippets do not detail the specific text of any regulations, the Law Explanation asserts that the modifications contradict 12 CFR § 563b.40. The change fits the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition, as it introduces ambiguity to a legal statement concerning the liquidation account, making it difficult to enforce the obligation to protect account holders in the event of liquidation.
